title: No Bath
lyrics:
[verse 1]
My duck don’t like the water—*splash!*
My toes don’t wanna go—*plop!*
The bubbles pop too loud—*pop!*
I’ll wash my knees—just watch me hop!

[chorus]
No bath! No way!
I’ll stay just like this!
No bath! No way!
I don’t need a wash!

[verse 2]
The towel’s too scratchy—*itch!*
The soap smells yucky—*ew!*
I’ll splash you if you come—*whoosh!*
I’m clean enough—just see me go!

[chorus]
No bath! No way!
I’ll stay just like this!
No bath! No way!
I don’t need a wash!

[ending]
No bath… no bath…
Okay… just one *splash*…
Then I’ll go… *crash!*
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, xylophone and tambourine, 125 BPM, bouncy beat, warm clear child vocal, sing each word clearly and slowly, fun and silly, not rushed
scene: The bathroom light flicks on at 7:15 PM, casting long shadows over the blue rubber duck and half-empty bubble bath bottle on the tub’s edge—Mom just peeled off your dinosaur pajama top and said, *"Time to wash the sand off your knees."*
cover_description: A tiny kid in dinosaur pajamas stands in the tub, splashing wildly as a rubber duck with an eye patch yells "NO BATH!" from the soap dish, bubbles bursting everywhere.
